input.counterSource: No credible counter-source found. The claim matches the spec definition directly.
input.evidence: The MDN source page at https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout was fetched directly and contains the supporting quote verbatim: "On the stacking axis, rather than sticking to a strict grid with gaps being left after shorter items, the items in the following row rise up to fill the gaps." The claim is a precise, accurate paraphrase of that text — not an overreach. MDN is Mozilla's official developer documentation and a primary authoritative source for CSS behavior. The page references Level 3 of the CSS Grid Layout specification, making this a spec-grounded claim, not marketing or forum speculation. No contradicting evidence exists — this is the definitional behavior of masonry layout and is consistent across all credible CSS documentation. The claim is also current: the MDN page references the modern `display: grid-lanes` and `inline-grid-lanes` values introduced in the updated CSS Grid Level 3 spec, indicating the content has been updated to reflect the latest specification state as of 2025-2026.
